name: Savor

verdict.text: Keep — no annual fee, unlimited 3% across dining, entertainment, streaming, and groceries is hard to beat for free

earn_rates.category: Grocery stores (excludes superstores like Walmart & Target)
Entertainment — movies, concerts, sporting events, amusement parks
Popular streaming services — Netflix, Hulu, Disney+
Hotels, vacation rentals & rental cars — via Capital One Travel

earn_note: Four uncapped 3% categories with no rotation or activation is unusually generous for a $0 card — it beats the Blue Cash Everyday's spending caps and matches the Freedom Unlimited's flat-rate simplicity while paying more on food and entertainment.

redemption_options.method: Statement credit / check / gift card / Amazon / PayPal

points.note: A straight cash-back currency, not miles — no transfer partners, no premium redemption to chase, always worth exactly 1¢.

transfer_partners.highlight: Cash back isn't transferable — it's a flat statement credit, check, gift card, or Capital One Travel/Entertainment redemption, always at face value.

insurance.detail: Secondary — pays after your personal auto insurance

protection_note: Thin by design — this is a cash-back card, not a travel card. No purchase protection or trip coverage of any kind; the one real protection is a modest $1,000-per-claim extended warranty.
rental_note: Secondary CDW only — decline the rental counter's own waiver and pay in full with this card to activate it, but your personal auto policy pays first.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Discover network transition
services.detail: New Savor accounts are being originated on the Discover network instead of Mastercard as of early 2026.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns the same cash-back rates (into your account)

additional_cards.note: No cost to add trusted users — an easy way to consolidate household dining/grocery spend onto the flat 3% categories.

timeline.text: Sign-up bonus temporarily increased to $250 (from $200) after $500 spend in 3 months — a limited-time offer, not the standing rate.
The Capital One-Uber partnership's unlimited 10% cash back on Uber/Uber Eats and complimentary Uber One membership expired; it is not part of the card's standing benefits.

The no-fee SavorOne card was renamed Savor, replacing an older fee-charging Savor (4% dining) product that was discontinued. This card is the same no-fee product as the old SavorOne, not a continuation of the original Savor.
